utilityName: Lee County Utilities Cross-Connection Control Program
dueBasis: Lee County Utilities says required devices are tested at installation and every year after that. The county uses a cross-connection portal, gives customers portal access tied to the account, and expects approved reports to keep the account in compliance.
coveredPropertyTypes: County utility customers with hazard conditions
Other service connections where Lee County requires protection

submissionMethods.label: Lee County Utilities cross-connection control and backflow prevention

penalties: Lee County Utilities keeps customers inside its cross-connection control program and portal workflow. Missing installation or annual testing leaves the account out of compliance until the accepted report is posted.
sourceExcerpt: Lee County is a useful Southwest Florida utility because it publishes installation-plus-annual testing, a county customer portal, and a real cross-connection workflow instead of generic water-quality copy.
verdictSummary: Lee County is a strong Southwest Florida utility because it combines annual testing, portal-driven compliance, and county-level cross-connection workflow on public pages.
whoIsAffected: Lee County Utilities customers with required backflow devices, especially irrigation, commercial, and other hazard-based service connections.
residentialNotes: Residential demand is strongest where irrigation or another county-triggered hazard puts the property into the program.
The county workflow is more useful than a generic local-plumber article because the customer portal matters.
commercialNotes: Commercial value is strong because the county portal becomes part of the actual next-action path.
Lee County is also a good metro anchor because it covers multiple city-branded searches under one county authority.

annualTesting.summary: Lee County says devices are tested at installation and annually thereafter inside the county cross-connection program.
highlights: Initial testing and annual testing are both explicit.
Lee County gives customers a dedicated portal path.
The account stays tied to county compliance until the accepted report is posted.
workflowSteps: Confirm the assembly is in Lee County Utilities' program.
Complete the installation or annual test.
Use the county portal and customer workflow to keep the record current.

irrigation.summary: Lee County is commercially useful for irrigation because county utility customers still need annual compliance and portal-based recordkeeping on irrigation assemblies.
highlights: Irrigation is one of the clearest local demand surfaces in this county program.
Portal-based compliance matters more than generic contractor selection.
The same installation and annual-testing logic still applies.
workflowSteps: Confirm the irrigation assembly is still inside Lee County's program.
Use the county portal to keep the account compliant.
Confirm the service connection is under Lee County Utilities' cross-connection program.
Complete the installation or annual test.
Use the county customer or reporting portal.
failureHighlights: Lee County says devices are tested at installation and annually thereafter.
The county uses a customer portal and a dedicated CCC portal.
County compliance is not complete until the accepted report is posted.

costBand.testingRange: Testing is market-priced, but the county portal and account workflow matter more than any public list price.
costBand.repairRetestRange: Repair and retest cost depends on the assembly and whether the corrective work delays county compliance.
costBand.pricingNotes: Lee County's local friction is the portal-driven compliance step, not just getting a device tested.

sources.label: Lee County Utilities cross-connection control and backflow prevention
